contents.value:
```redis
SET key value [EX seconds] [PX ms] [EXAT ts] [PXAT ms-ts] [NX|XX] [KEEPTTL] [GET]
```
**SET** assigns a string value to a key. Options:
- `EX`/`PX` — set TTL in seconds/milliseconds
- `NX` — only set if key does **not** exist (distributed lock)
- `XX` — only set if key **already** exists
- `GET` — return the old value (Redis 6.2+)
- `KEEPTTL` — retain existing TTL

```redis
SET session:abc "token" EX 3600 NX
SET counter 0
SET key val XX GET
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
GET key
```
**GET** returns the string value of `key`. Returns `nil` if the key does not exist. Returns an error if the value is not a string.

```redis
SET greeting "hello"
GET greeting  # => "hello"
GET nonexistent  # => (nil)
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
MSET key1 value1 [key2 value2 ...]
```
**MSET** sets multiple key-value pairs atomically. Never fails — always OK.

```redis
MSET first "Hello" second "World"
MGET first second  # => "Hello" "World"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is the number of keys

```redis
MGET key1 [key2 ...]
```
**MGET** returns values for all specified keys. Non-existing keys return `nil`.

```redis
MGET key1 key2 key3
# => "val1" (nil) "val3"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
INCR key
```
**INCR** atomically increments the integer stored at `key` by 1. If the key does not exist, it's set to 0 before incrementing. Returns the new value.

```redis
SET counter 10
INCR counter  # => 11
INCR newcounter  # => 1
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**. Common for counters, rate limiters.

```redis
INCRBY key increment
```
**INCRBY** atomically increments the integer at `key` by the given amount. Can be negative for decrements.

```redis
SET counter 10
INCRBY counter 5  # => 15
INCRBY counter -3  # => 12
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
DECR key
```
**DECR** atomically decrements the integer at `key` by 1.

```redis
SET counter 10
DECR counter  # => 9
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
APPEND key value
```
**APPEND** appends `value` to the existing string at `key`. Creates the key if it doesn't exist. Returns the length of the string after appending.

```redis
APPEND mykey "Hello"
APPEND mykey " World"
GET mykey  # => "Hello World"
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** amortized

```redis
STRLEN key
```
**STRLEN** returns the length of the string stored at `key`. Returns 0 if the key doesn't exist.

```redis
SET mykey "Hello World"
STRLEN mykey  # => 11
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
GETRANGE key start end
```
**GETRANGE** returns a substring of the string at `key`. Negative offsets count from the end.

```redis
SET mykey "Hello, World"
GETRANGE mykey 0 4  # => "Hello"
GETRANGE mykey -5 -1  # => "World"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is the length of the returned string

```redis
SETEX key seconds value
```
**SETEX** sets `key` to `value` with an expiration in seconds. Atomic equivalent of `SET key value` + `EXPIRE key seconds`.

```redis
SETEX session 3600 "data"
TTL session  # => 3600
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
SETNX key value
```
**SETNX** (SET if Not eXists) sets `key` to `value` only if it doesn't already exist. Returns 1 if set, 0 otherwise.

```redis
SETNX mykey "Hello"  # => 1
SETNX mykey "World"  # => 0 (key exists)
```
**Note**: For distributed locks, prefer `SET key value NX EX seconds`.
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
GETDEL key
```
**GETDEL** gets the value and deletes the key atomically (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
SET temp "data"
GETDEL temp  # => "data"
GET temp  # => (nil)
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
GETEX key [EX seconds | PX ms | EXAT ts | PXAT ms-ts | PERSIST]
```
**GETEX** gets the value and optionally sets/removes its expiration (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
GETEX mykey EX 100  # Get and set 100s TTL
GETEX mykey PERSIST  # Get and remove TTL
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
INCRBYFLOAT key increment
```
**INCRBYFLOAT** increments the floating point number stored at `key`.

```redis
SET price 10.50
INCRBYFLOAT price 0.1  # => "10.6"
INCRBYFLOAT price -5  # => "5.6"
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
LCS key1 key2 [LEN] [IDX] [MINMATCHLEN len] [WITHMATCHLEN]
```
**LCS** finds the Longest Common Substring between two string keys (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
SET key1 "ohmytext" 
SET key2 "mynewtext"
LCS key1 key2  # => "mytext"
LCS key1 key2 LEN  # => 6
```
Time complexity: **O(N*M)** where N and M are string lengths

```redis
HSET key field value [field value ...]
```
**HSET** sets field(s) in a hash. Creates the hash if it doesn't exist. Returns the number of new fields added.

```redis
HSET user:1 name "Alice" age "30" email "alice@example.com"
HGET user:1 name  # => "Alice"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of field-value pairs

```redis
HGET key field
```
**HGET** returns the value of a field in a hash. Returns `nil` if field or key doesn't exist.

```redis
HSET user:1 name "Alice"
HGET user:1 name  # => "Alice"
HGET user:1 missing  # => (nil)
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
HGETALL key
```
**HGETALL** returns all fields and values in a hash as a flat list.

```redis
HSET user:1 name "Alice" age "30"
HGETALL user:1
# => 1) "name" 2) "Alice" 3) "age" 4) "30"
```
**Warning**: For large hashes, consider `HSCAN`.
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
HDEL key field [field ...]
```
**HDEL** removes fields from a hash. Returns the number of fields removed.

```redis
HDEL user:1 age email
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of fields to remove

```redis
HEXISTS key field
```
**HEXISTS** checks if a field exists in a hash. Returns 1 if exists, 0 otherwise.

```redis
HEXISTS user:1 name  # => 1
HEXISTS user:1 missing  # => 0
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
HMSET key field value [field value ...]
```
**HMSET** sets multiple fields in a hash. **Deprecated** in favor of `HSET` which now accepts multiple field-value pairs.

```redis
HMSET user:1 name "Alice" age "30"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
HMGET key field [field ...]
```
**HMGET** returns values for multiple fields. Non-existing fields return `nil`.

```redis
HMGET user:1 name age missing
# => "Alice" "30" (nil)
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
HINCRBY key field increment
```
**HINCRBY** increments the integer value of a hash field. Creates the field with value 0 if it doesn't exist.

```redis
HINCRBY user:1 login_count 1
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
HLEN key
```
**HLEN** returns the number of fields in a hash.

```redis
HLEN user:1  # => 3
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
HRANDFIELD key [count [WITHVALUES]]
```
**HRANDFIELD** returns random field(s) from a hash (Redis 6.2+). Negative count allows duplicates.

```redis
HRANDFIELD user:1 2 WITHVALUES
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is count

```redis
HSCAN key cursor [MATCH pattern] [COUNT count]
```
**HSCAN** incrementally iterates hash fields. Use instead of `HGETALL` for large hashes.

```redis
HSCAN user:1 0 MATCH name* COUNT 10
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** per call, **O(N)** for full iteration

```redis
LPUSH key element [element ...]
```
**LPUSH** inserts elements at the head (left) of a list. Creates the list if it doesn't exist. Elements are inserted left to right, so the last specified is the first in the list.

```redis
LPUSH mylist "world" "hello"
LRANGE mylist 0 -1  # => "hello" "world"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of elements

```redis
RPUSH key element [element ...]
```
**RPUSH** inserts elements at the tail (right) of a list.

```redis
RPUSH mylist "hello" "world"
LRANGE mylist 0 -1  # => "hello" "world"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
LPOP key [count]
```
**LPOP** removes and returns the first element(s) from a list. With `count`, returns up to that many elements (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
LPOP mylist  # => "hello"
LPOP mylist 3  # => multiple elements
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is count

```redis
RPOP key [count]
```
**RPOP** removes and returns the last element(s) from a list.

```redis
RPOP mylist  # => "world"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
LRANGE key start stop
```
**LRANGE** returns elements from a list in the specified range. 0 is the first, -1 is the last.

```redis
LRANGE mylist 0 -1  # All elements
LRANGE mylist 0 9   # First 10
```
Time complexity: **O(S+N)** where S is start offset and N is range length

```redis
LLEN key
```
**LLEN** returns the length of a list. Returns 0 if key doesn't exist.

```redis
LLEN mylist  # => 5
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
LINDEX key index
```
**LINDEX** returns the element at `index` in a list. Negative indices count from the tail.

```redis
LINDEX mylist 0   # First element
LINDEX mylist -1  # Last element
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is the number of elements to traverse

```redis
LINSERT key BEFORE|AFTER pivot element
```
**LINSERT** inserts an element before or after the pivot value.

```redis
LINSERT mylist BEFORE "World" "There"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of elements to traverse

```redis
BLPOP key [key ...] timeout
```
**BLPOP** is the blocking version of LPOP. Blocks until an element is available or timeout is reached. Timeout of 0 blocks indefinitely.

```redis
BLPOP queue:tasks 30
BLPOP q1 q2 q3 0  # Check multiple queues
```
Used in **message queue** patterns.
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of keys

```redis
BRPOP key [key ...] timeout
```
**BRPOP** is the blocking version of RPOP. Blocks until an element is available.

```redis
BRPOP queue:tasks 30
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
LMOVE source destination LEFT|RIGHT LEFT|RIGHT
```
**LMOVE** atomically pops from one end of source and pushes to one end of destination (Redis 6.2+). Replaces RPOPLPUSH.

```redis
LMOVE src dst LEFT RIGHT  # Move head of src to tail of dst
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
LPOS key element [RANK rank] [COUNT count] [MAXLEN maxlen]
```
**LPOS** returns the position of an element in a list (Redis 6.0.6+).

```redis
LPOS mylist "hello"  # => 0
LPOS mylist "hello" RANK 2  # Second occurrence
LPOS mylist "hello" COUNT 0  # All positions
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
SADD key member [member ...]
```
**SADD** adds members to a set. Ignores members that already exist. Returns the number of new members added.

```redis
SADD tags "redis" "database" "nosql"
SMEMBERS tags  # => "redis" "database" "nosql"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of members

```redis
SREM key member [member ...]
```
**SREM** removes members from a set. Returns the number of members removed.

```redis
SREM tags "nosql"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
SMEMBERS key
```
**SMEMBERS** returns all members of a set. For large sets, use `SSCAN`.

```redis
SMEMBERS tags  # => "redis" "database"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
SISMEMBER key member
```
**SISMEMBER** checks if `member` is in the set. Returns 1 or 0.

```redis
SISMEMBER tags "redis"  # => 1
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
SCARD key
```
**SCARD** returns the number of elements in a set (cardinality).

```redis
SCARD tags  # => 3
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
SUNION key [key ...]
```
**SUNION** returns the union of multiple sets.

```redis
SUNION set1 set2  # Members in either set
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is total elements across all sets

```redis
SINTER key [key ...]
```
**SINTER** returns the intersection of multiple sets.

```redis
SINTER set1 set2  # Members in both sets
```
Time complexity: **O(N*M)** worst case

```redis
SDIFF key [key ...]
```
**SDIFF** returns members in the first set but not in subsequent sets.

```redis
SDIFF set1 set2  # Members only in set1
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
SPOP key [count]
```
**SPOP** removes and returns random member(s) from a set.

```redis
SPOP myset  # Random member
SPOP myset 3  # 3 random members
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is count

```redis
SMOVE source destination member
```
**SMOVE** atomically moves a member from one set to another.

```redis
SMOVE active archived "item1"
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
SINTERCARD numkeys key [key ...] [LIMIT limit]
```
**SINTERCARD** returns the cardinality of the intersection without computing the full result (Redis 7.0+). Use `LIMIT` to stop early.

```redis
SINTERCARD 2 set1 set2 LIMIT 100
```
Time complexity: **O(N*M)** worst case

```redis
ZADD key [NX|XX] [GT|LT] [CH] score member [score member ...]
```
**ZADD** adds members with scores to a sorted set. Options:
- `NX` — only add new elements
- `XX` — only update existing elements
- `GT` — only update if new score > current
- `LT` — only update if new score < current
- `CH` — return number of changed elements

```redis
ZADD leaderboard 100 "alice" 200 "bob"
ZADD leaderboard GT CH 250 "alice"  # Update if higher
```
Time complexity: **O(log(N))** per element

```redis
ZRANGE key min max [BYSCORE|BYLEX] [REV] [LIMIT offset count] [WITHSCORES]
```
**ZRANGE** returns members in a sorted set. Since Redis 6.2, replaces ZRANGEBYSCORE, ZRANGEBYLEX, ZREVRANGE.

```redis
ZRANGE lb 0 9 WITHSCORES  # Top 10 by rank
ZRANGE lb 100 200 BYSCORE LIMIT 0 10  # By score range
ZRANGE lb 0 9 REV WITHSCORES  # Reverse order
```
Time complexity: **O(log(N)+M)** where M is the result set size

```redis
ZSCORE key member
```
**ZSCORE** returns the score of a member. Returns `nil` if member or key doesn't exist.

```redis
ZSCORE leaderboard "alice"  # => "100"
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
ZRANK key member [WITHSCORE]
```
**ZRANK** returns the rank (0-based, ascending by score) of a member. Returns `nil` if not found.

```redis
ZRANK leaderboard "alice"  # => 0 (lowest score)
ZRANK leaderboard "alice" WITHSCORE  # (Redis 7.2+)
```
Time complexity: **O(log(N))**

```redis
ZINCRBY key increment member
```
**ZINCRBY** increments the score of a member. Creates the member with score if it doesn't exist.

```redis
ZINCRBY leaderboard 10 "alice"  # => new score
```
Time complexity: **O(log(N))**

```redis
ZCARD key
```
**ZCARD** returns the number of members in a sorted set.

```redis
ZCARD leaderboard  # => 5
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
ZCOUNT key min max
```
**ZCOUNT** counts members with scores in the given range. Use `-inf` and `+inf` for unbounded.

```redis
ZCOUNT leaderboard 100 200
ZCOUNT leaderboard -inf +inf  # All
ZCOUNT leaderboard "(100" 200  # Exclusive min
```
Time complexity: **O(log(N))**

```redis
ZPOPMIN key [count]
```
**ZPOPMIN** removes and returns member(s) with the lowest scores.

```redis
ZPOPMIN leaderboard 3
```
Useful for **priority queues**.
Time complexity: **O(log(N)*M)** where M is count

```redis
ZPOPMAX key [count]
```
**ZPOPMAX** removes and returns member(s) with the highest scores.

```redis
ZPOPMAX leaderboard 3
```
Time complexity: **O(log(N)*M)**

```redis
ZUNIONSTORE dest numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS w1 w2 ...] [AGGREGATE SUM|MIN|MAX]
```
**ZUNIONSTORE** stores the union of sorted sets with optional score weighting.

```redis
ZUNIONSTORE combined 2 zset1 zset2 WEIGHTS 2 1 AGGREGATE MAX
```
Time complexity: **O(N*log(N))** with N being the sum of sizes

```redis
ZINTERSTORE dest numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS w1 w2 ...] [AGGREGATE SUM|MIN|MAX]
```
**ZINTERSTORE** stores the intersection of sorted sets.

```redis
ZINTERSTORE common 2 zset1 zset2
```
Time complexity: **O(N*K*log(N))** where K is number of keys

```redis
DEL key [key ...]
```
**DEL** removes keys synchronously. Returns the number of keys removed.

```redis
DEL key1 key2 key3  # => 3
```
**Note**: For non-blocking deletion of large keys, use `UNLINK`.
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of keys

```redis
UNLINK key [key ...]
```
**UNLINK** removes keys asynchronously (non-blocking). The actual memory reclamation happens in a background thread.

```redis
UNLINK bigkey1 bigkey2
```
**Preferred over DEL** for large values (big lists, sets, hashes).
Time complexity: **O(1)** for each key, **O(N)** for reclamation in background

```redis
EXISTS key [key ...]
```
**EXISTS** checks if key(s) exist. Returns the count of existing keys.

```redis
EXISTS key1  # => 1
EXISTS key1 key2 key3  # => 2 (two exist)
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of keys

```redis
TYPE key
```
**TYPE** returns the data type of the value at `key`: string, list, set, zset, hash, stream.

```redis
SET mykey "hello"
TYPE mykey  # => string
LPUSH mylist "a"
TYPE mylist  # => list
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
EXPIRE key seconds [NX|XX|GT|LT]
```
**EXPIRE** sets a timeout on a key in seconds. Options (Redis 7.0+):
- `NX` — set expiry only if key has no expiry
- `XX` — set expiry only if key has an existing expiry
- `GT` — set only if new expiry > current
- `LT` — set only if new expiry < current

```redis
SET mykey "hello"
EXPIRE mykey 300
TTL mykey  # => 300
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
TTL key
```
**TTL** returns remaining time to live in seconds. Returns -2 if key doesn't exist, -1 if key has no expiry.

```redis
SET mykey "hello" EX 60
TTL mykey  # => 60
TTL nonexistent  # => -2
TTL persistent_key  # => -1
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
PTTL key
```
**PTTL** returns remaining TTL in **milliseconds**. Returns -2 if key doesn't exist, -1 if no expiry.

```redis
PTTL mykey  # => 59500
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
PERSIST key
```
**PERSIST** removes the expiration from a key, making it persistent.

```redis
SET mykey "hello" EX 60
PERSIST mykey
TTL mykey  # => -1
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
KEYS pattern
```
**KEYS** finds all keys matching a glob pattern.

⚠️ **Warning**: `KEYS *` blocks the server on large databases. Use `SCAN` in production.

```redis
KEYS *  # All keys
KEYS user:*  # Keys starting with user:
KEYS h?llo  # hello, hallo, hxllo...
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
SCAN cursor [MATCH pattern] [COUNT hint] [TYPE type]
```
**SCAN** incrementally iterates the key space. Non-blocking alternative to `KEYS`. Returns a cursor and a batch of keys.

```redis
SCAN 0 MATCH user:* COUNT 100
SCAN 0 TYPE string  # Only string keys
```
Iterate until cursor returns 0.
Time complexity: **O(1)** per call, **O(N)** for full iteration

```redis
RENAME key newkey
```
**RENAME** renames a key. Overwrites `newkey` if it already exists.

```redis
RENAME old_name new_name
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
DUMP key
```
**DUMP** serializes the value at `key` in a Redis-specific format. Use with `RESTORE` for key migration.

```redis
DUMP mykey
# Returns binary encoded value
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** to access, **O(N*M)** to serialize where M is serialized size

```redis
RESTORE key ttl serialized-value [REPLACE] [ABSTTL] [IDLETIME seconds] [FREQ frequency]
```
**RESTORE** deserializes a value from `DUMP`. Use `REPLACE` to overwrite existing key.

```redis
DUMP mykey  # on source
RESTORE mykey 0 "\x00..." REPLACE  # on destination
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** to access, **O(N*M)** to reconstruct

```redis
COPY source destination [DB db] [REPLACE]
```
**COPY** copies a key's value to another key (Redis 6.2+). Use `REPLACE` to overwrite destination.

```redis
COPY src dst REPLACE
COPY src dst DB 1  # Copy to different database
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is the size of the value

```redis
SORT key [BY pattern] [LIMIT offset count] [GET pattern ...] [ASC|DESC] [ALPHA] [STORE dest]
```
**SORT** sorts elements in a list, set, or sorted set. Can dereference external keys.

```redis
SORT mylist ALPHA DESC LIMIT 0 10
SORT mylist BY weight_* GET # GET name_* STORE sorted
```
Time complexity: **O(N+M*log(M))** where N is list size, M is number of returned elements

```redis
OBJECT ENCODING key
```
**OBJECT ENCODING** returns the internal encoding used by Redis for the value.

```redis
OBJECT ENCODING mykey
# Returns: embstr, int, raw, ziplist, listpack, quicklist,
# hashtable, skiplist, intset, etc.
```
Useful for understanding memory optimization.
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
RANDOMKEY
```
**RANDOMKEY** returns a random key from the current database. Returns `nil` if the database is empty.

```redis
RANDOMKEY  # => "user:42"
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
PFADD key element [element ...]
```
**PFADD** adds elements to a HyperLogLog. Uses ~12KB of memory regardless of cardinality.

```redis
PFADD unique_visitors "user1" "user2" "user3"
PFCOUNT unique_visitors  # Approximate unique count
```
Ideal for **counting unique items** (visitors, IPs, etc.) with 0.81% standard error.
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
PFCOUNT key [key ...]
```
**PFCOUNT** returns the approximate cardinality of a HyperLogLog. With multiple keys, returns the cardinality of the union.

```redis
PFCOUNT visitors  # => ~1000000
PFCOUNT day1 day2 day3  # Union of all three
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** for single key, **O(N)** for union

```redis
PFMERGE destkey sourcekey [sourcekey ...]
```
**PFMERGE** merges multiple HyperLogLogs into one.

```redis
PFMERGE weekly_visitors mon tue wed thu fri
PFCOUNT weekly_visitors
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of source keys

```redis
SETBIT key offset value
```
**SETBIT** sets or clears the bit at `offset`. Returns the original bit value.

```redis
SETBIT active_users 1000 1  # User 1000 is active
GETBIT active_users 1000  # => 1
```
Useful for **flags, presence tracking, feature flags**.
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
GETBIT key offset
```
**GETBIT** returns the bit value at `offset`.

```redis
GETBIT active_users 1000  # => 1 (active)
GETBIT active_users 9999  # => 0 (not set)
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
BITCOUNT key [start end [BYTE|BIT]]
```
**BITCOUNT** counts the number of set bits (1s). Optional range specifies byte or bit range.

```redis
BITCOUNT active_users  # Total active users
BITCOUNT active_users 0 0  # First byte
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
BITOP operation destkey key [key ...]
```
**BITOP** performs bitwise operations (AND, OR, XOR, NOT) between strings.

```redis
BITOP AND both_active day1_active day2_active
BITOP OR any_active day1_active day2_active
```
Useful for **combining bitmap flags**.
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
XADD key [NOMKSTREAM] [MAXLEN|MINID [=|~] threshold] *|id field value [field value ...]
```
**XADD** appends an entry to a stream. Use `*` for auto-generated ID.

```redis
XADD mystream * sensor_id 1234 temperature 19.8
XADD mystream MAXLEN ~ 1000 * data value  # Cap at ~1000 entries
```
Streams are the Redis data structure for **log/event processing**.
Time complexity: **O(1)** when ID is auto-generated

```redis
XREAD [COUNT count] [BLOCK ms] STREAMS key [key ...] id [id ...]
```
**XREAD** reads entries from one or more streams. Use `$` to read only new entries.

```redis
XREAD COUNT 10 STREAMS mystream 0  # From beginning
XREAD BLOCK 5000 COUNT 10 STREAMS mystream $  # Block for new entries
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is count

```redis
XREADGROUP GROUP group consumer [COUNT count] [BLOCK ms] [NOACK] STREAMS key [key ...] id [id ...]
```
**XREADGROUP** reads from a stream as a consumer group member. Use `>` to read only undelivered messages.

```redis
XREADGROUP GROUP mygroup consumer1 COUNT 10 BLOCK 5000 STREAMS mystream >
```
Consumer groups enable **reliable message processing** with acknowledgments.
Time complexity: **O(M)** where M is count

```redis
XACK key group id [id ...]
```
**XACK** acknowledges messages, removing them from the consumer's pending entries list (PEL).

```redis
XACK mystream mygroup 1526569495631-0
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** per ID

```redis
XGROUP CREATE key group id|$ [MKSTREAM] [ENTRIESREAD entries-read]
```
**XGROUP CREATE** creates a consumer group.
- Use `$` to consume only new messages
- Use `0` to consume from the beginning
- `MKSTREAM` creates the stream if it doesn't exist

```redis
XGROUP CREATE events processors $ MKSTREAM
XGROUP CREATE events reprocessors 0
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
XPENDING key group [[IDLE min-idle-time] start end count [consumer]]
```
**XPENDING** inspects the pending entries list — messages delivered but not yet acknowledged.

```redis
XPENDING mystream mygroup  # Summary
XPENDING mystream mygroup - + 10  # First 10 pending
XPENDING mystream mygroup IDLE 60000 - + 10  # Idle > 60s
```
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
XCLAIM key group consumer min-idle-time id [id ...] [IDLE ms] [TIME ms] [RETRYCOUNT count] [FORCE] [JUSTID]
```
**XCLAIM** transfers ownership of pending messages to another consumer. Used for **message recovery** when a consumer crashes.

```redis
XCLAIM mystream mygroup consumer2 3600000 1526569495631-0
```
Time complexity: **O(log(N))** per message

```redis
XAUTOCLAIM key group consumer min-idle-time start [COUNT count] [JUSTID]
```
**XAUTOCLAIM** automatically transfers idle pending messages (Redis 6.2+). Simpler than XPENDING + XCLAIM.

```redis
XAUTOCLAIM mystream mygroup consumer2 3600000 0-0 COUNT 25
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** per claimed message

```redis
XINFO STREAM key [FULL [COUNT count]]
```
**XINFO STREAM** returns information about a stream including length, first/last entry, consumer groups.

```redis
XINFO STREAM mystream
XINFO STREAM mystream FULL  # Detailed view
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** for default, **O(N)** for FULL

```redis
XLEN key
```
**XLEN** returns the number of entries in a stream.

```redis
XLEN mystream  # => 150
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
XTRIM key MAXLEN|MINID [=|~] threshold
```
**XTRIM** trims a stream to a maximum length or minimum ID. Use `~` for approximate trimming (faster).

```redis
XTRIM mystream MAXLEN ~ 1000
XTRIM mystream MINID 1526569495631-0
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is the number of evicted entries

```redis
SUBSCRIBE channel [channel ...]
```
**SUBSCRIBE** listens for messages on one or more channels. The connection enters subscriber mode — only (P|S)SUBSCRIBE, (P|S)UNSUBSCRIBE, PING, RESET, QUIT are allowed.

```redis
SUBSCRIBE notifications alerts
```
Messages are fire-and-forget — no persistence, no replay.
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of channels

```redis
PUBLISH channel message
```
**PUBLISH** sends a message to all subscribers of a channel. Returns the number of clients that received it.

```redis
PUBLISH notifications "New order received"
```
Time complexity: **O(N+M)** where N is channel subscribers and M is pattern subscribers

```redis
PSUBSCRIBE pattern [pattern ...]
```
**PSUBSCRIBE** subscribes to channels matching glob patterns.

```redis
PSUBSCRIBE news.*  # news.sports, news.tech, etc.
PSUBSCRIBE __keyevent@0__:*  # Keyspace notifications
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of patterns

```redis
MULTI
```
**MULTI** marks the start of a transaction block. Subsequent commands are queued and executed atomically with `EXEC`.

```redis
MULTI
SET key1 "val1"
SET key2 "val2"
INCR counter
EXEC  # => [OK, OK, 1]
```
Transactions are **atomic** — either all commands execute or none.
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
EXEC
```
**EXEC** executes all queued commands in the transaction and restores the connection to normal. Returns an array of results.

Returns `nil` if `WATCH` detected a modification (optimistic lock failure).

```redis
WATCH balance
MULTI
DECRBY balance 100
INCRBY other_balance 100
EXEC  # nil if balance was modified
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of queued commands

```redis
WATCH key [key ...]
```
**WATCH** enables optimistic locking (check-and-set). If any watched key is modified before `EXEC`, the transaction is aborted.

```redis
WATCH balance
val = GET balance
MULTI
SET balance (val - 100)
EXEC  # nil if another client changed balance
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** per key

```redis
DISCARD
```
**DISCARD** aborts the transaction, discarding all queued commands.

```redis
MULTI
SET key1 "val1"
DISCARD  # Transaction cancelled
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of queued commands

```redis
EVAL script numkeys [key ...] [arg ...]
```
**EVAL** executes a Lua script server-side. Scripts are **atomic** — no other command runs during execution.

```redis
EVAL "return redis.call('GET',KEYS[1])" 1 mykey
EVAL "redis.call('SET',KEYS[1],ARGV[1]); return redis.call('GET',KEYS[1])" 1 mykey myval
```
Use `redis.call()` for errors, `redis.pcall()` for protected calls.
Time complexity: Depends on the script

```redis
EVALSHA sha1 numkeys [key ...] [arg ...]
```
**EVALSHA** executes a cached Lua script by its SHA1 hash. Load scripts with `SCRIPT LOAD` first.

```redis
SCRIPT LOAD "return redis.call('GET',KEYS[1])"  # => sha1
EVALSHA sha1 1 mykey
```
More efficient than EVAL for repeated scripts.
Time complexity: Depends on the script

```redis
FUNCTION LOAD [REPLACE] function-code
```
**FUNCTION LOAD** loads a function library (Redis 7.0+). Functions persist across restarts unlike EVAL scripts.

```redis
FUNCTION LOAD "#!lua name=mylib\nredis.register_function('myfunc', function(keys, args) return redis.call('GET', keys[1]) end)"
FCALL myfunc 1 mykey
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is the function code length

```redis
FCALL function numkeys [key ...] [arg ...]
```
**FCALL** calls a loaded Redis function (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
FCALL myfunc 1 mykey
```
Time complexity: Depends on the function

```redis
GEOADD key [NX|XX] [CH] longitude latitude member [longitude latitude member ...]
```
**GEOADD** adds geospatial items (longitude/latitude) to a sorted set.

```redis
GEOADD restaurants 13.361389 38.115556 "Sicily Pizza"
GEOADD restaurants NX 2.349014 48.864716 "Paris Cafe"
```
Internally uses a sorted set with geohash scores.
Time complexity: **O(log(N))** per item

```redis
GEOSEARCH key FROMMEMBER member|FROMLONLAT lon lat BYRADIUS radius m|km|ft|mi|BYBOX width height m|km|ft|mi [ASC|DESC] [COUNT count [ANY]] [WITHCOORD] [WITHDIST] [WITHHASH]
```
**GEOSEARCH** finds members within a radius or bounding box (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
GEOSEARCH places FROMLONLAT 13.361 38.115 BYRADIUS 100 km ASC COUNT 5 WITHDIST
GEOSEARCH places FROMMEMBER "home" BYBOX 400 400 km ASC
```
Time complexity: **O(N+log(N))** where N is the number of nearby members

```redis
GEODIST key member1 member2 [M|KM|FT|MI]
```
**GEODIST** returns the distance between two geospatial members.

```redis
GEODIST places "Rome" "Paris" km  # => "1105.5914"
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
INFO [section [section ...]]
```
**INFO** returns server information and statistics. Sections: server, clients, memory, persistence, stats, replication, cpu, commandstats, latencystats, cluster, keyspace, modules, errorstats.

```redis
INFO  # All sections
INFO memory  # Memory usage details
INFO server  # Server version, uptime, etc.
INFO keyspace  # Per-database key counts
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
CONFIG GET parameter [parameter ...]
```
**CONFIG GET** reads configuration parameters. Supports glob patterns.

```redis
CONFIG GET maxmemory
CONFIG GET *policy*
CONFIG GET save
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of parameters

```redis
CONFIG SET parameter value [parameter value ...]
```
**CONFIG SET** dynamically changes configuration without restart.

```redis
CONFIG SET maxmemory 256mb
CONFIG SET maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
CONFIG SET save "3600 1 300 100"
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** for config parameters requiring list processing

```redis
FLUSHDB [ASYNC|SYNC]
```
**FLUSHDB** deletes all keys in the current database.

⚠️ **Destructive operation** — use with caution in production.

```redis
FLUSHDB  # Synchronous, blocks server
FLUSHDB ASYNC  # Non-blocking
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of keys

```redis
FLUSHALL [ASYNC|SYNC]
```
**FLUSHALL** deletes all keys from **all databases**.

⚠️ **Destructive operation** — deletes everything.

```redis
FLUSHALL ASYNC
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is total keys across all databases

```redis
BGSAVE [SCHEDULE]
```
**BGSAVE** triggers a background RDB snapshot. Returns error if another save is in progress (unless `SCHEDULE` is used).

```redis
BGSAVE
BGSAVE SCHEDULE  # Schedule if another save is running
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** to trigger

```redis
DBSIZE
```
**DBSIZE** returns the number of keys in the currently selected database.

```redis
DBSIZE  # => (integer) 42
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
SLOWLOG GET [count]
```
**SLOWLOG GET** returns entries from the slow log — commands that exceeded the `slowlog-log-slower-than` threshold.

```redis
SLOWLOG GET 10
```
Each entry contains: ID, timestamp, execution time (μs), command + args, client info.
Time complexity: **O(N)**

```redis
CLIENT LIST [TYPE normal|master|replica|pubsub] [ID id ...]
```
**CLIENT LIST** returns info about connected clients: address, fd, age, idle time, flags, database, command.

```redis
CLIENT LIST
CLIENT LIST TYPE normal
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of clients

```redis
MEMORY USAGE key [SAMPLES count]
```
**MEMORY USAGE** estimates memory consumption of a key in bytes, including overhead.

```redis
MEMORY USAGE mykey  # => (integer) 56
MEMORY USAGE myhash SAMPLES 5  # More accurate for complex types
```
Time complexity: **O(1)** for simple keys, **O(N)** with SAMPLES for complex types

```redis
MONITOR
```
**MONITOR** streams every command processed by the server in real-time. Useful for debugging.

```redis
MONITOR
# Output: 1339518083.107412 [0 127.0.0.1:60866] "keys" "*"
```
⚠️ **Performance impact** — reduces throughput by ~50%. Never use in production.

```redis
SELECT index
```
**SELECT** switches to a different database (0-15 by default).

```redis
SELECT 1  # Switch to database 1
SET mykey "hello"
SELECT 0  # Back to database 0
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
ACL SETUSER username [rule [rule ...]]
```
**ACL SETUSER** creates or modifies a user's access control rules.

Rules:
- `on`/`off` — enable/disable user
- `>password` — add password
- `~pattern` — allowed key patterns
- `+command` — allow command
- `-command` — deny command
- `+@category` — allow command category
- `allkeys` — allow all keys
- `allcommands` — allow all commands

```redis
ACL SETUSER readonly on >pass123 ~cached:* +get +mget +hgetall
ACL SETUSER admin on >adminpass allkeys allcommands
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is number of rules

```redis
AUTH [username] password
```
**AUTH** authenticates to the server. With ACL (Redis 6+), provide username and password.

```redis
AUTH mypassword  # Default user
AUTH myuser mypassword  # ACL user
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is the number of passwords configured

```redis
CLUSTER INFO
```
**CLUSTER INFO** returns cluster state: enabled, state (ok/fail), known nodes, size, slots assigned, messages sent/received.

```redis
CLUSTER INFO
# cluster_enabled:1
# cluster_state:ok
# cluster_slots_assigned:16384
# cluster_size:3
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
CLUSTER NODES
```
**CLUSTER NODES** returns the cluster membership table. Each line describes a node: ID, address, flags, master, ping/pong times, epoch, link-state, slots.

```redis
CLUSTER NODES
# 07c37df... 127.0.0.1:6379 myself,master - 0 0 1 connected 0-5460
```
Time complexity: **O(N)** where N is the number of nodes

```redis
CLUSTER FAILOVER [FORCE|TAKEOVER]
```
**CLUSTER FAILOVER** initiates manual failover of a master. Run on the replica.

- No option: safe failover with coordination
- `FORCE`: force failover even if master is unreachable
- `TAKEOVER`: force without cluster consensus

```redis
CLUSTER FAILOVER  # Safe failover
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
REPLICAOF host port
REPLICAOF NO ONE
```
**REPLICAOF** makes the server a replica of another or promotes it to master.

```redis
REPLICAOF 192.168.1.1 6379  # Become replica
REPLICAOF NO ONE  # Promote to master
```
Time complexity: **O(1)**

```redis
PING [message]
```
**PING** tests connectivity. Returns `PONG` or echoes the message.

```redis
PING  # => PONG
PING "hello"  # => "hello"
```
Commonly used for health checks and keepalive.
Time complexity: **O(1)**
